facts.text: The ocean covers approximately 70.8% of Earth's surface.
The ocean contains 97% of Earth's water.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean by area.
The five oceans in descending order by area are: Pacific, Atlantic, Indian, Antarctic/Southern, and Arctic.
Life within the ocean evolved 3 billion years prior to life on land.
The ocean still supplies approximately half of Earth's atmospheric oxygen.
The ocean originated photosynthesis on Earth.
Seawater covers about 361,000,000 km² of Earth's surface.
Point Nemo is the ocean's furthest pole of inaccessibility, located in the South Pacific Ocean.
Point Nemo is roughly 2,688 km from the nearest land.
The region around Point Nemo is known as the 'spacecraft cemetery' of the South Pacific Ocean.
Point Nemo is located at coordinates 48°52.6′S 123°23.6′W.
In Earth's early history, the ocean is thought to have possibly covered Earth completely.
Earth's surface can be divided into land and water hemispheres due to the ocean's uneven distribution.
Comb jellies, Brachiopods, and Echinoderms are animal groups found only in marine environments.
Cetaceans — whales, dolphins, and porpoises — primarily live in the ocean.
Seabirds spend most of their lifetime on water, going on land only for breeding.
Seven species of turtles are sea turtles.
Containerization greatly boosted the efficiency and reduced the cost of shipping products by sea.
Containerization was a major factor in the rise of globalization and exponential increases in international trade in the mid-to-late 20th century.
Ocean scientists split the ocean into both vertical and horizontal zones based on physical and biological conditions.
The ocean acts as a huge reservoir of heat for Earth's energy budget.
The ocean is a primary component of Earth's carbon cycle and water cycle.
Most animal phyla have species that inhabit the ocean.
Both the depth and distance from shore strongly influence the biodiversity of ocean regions.
In 2021, 43 expert scientists published the first scientific framework for ocean protection.
Marine protected areas (MPAs) are one of the key measures used for ocean protection.
Offshore drilling and deep sea mining are classified as extractive industries using the ocean.
The ocean forms the basis for climate and weather patterns worldwide.
Desalination of ocean water is used as a method of freshwater production.
